heading: Authentication & Subscription

heading: 1password Skill
content: Query and retrieve items from 1Password vaults using the 1Password CLI for secure credential access.. You can query 1Password vaults to retrieve credentials, secure notes, API keys, and other secrets using the `op` CLI. This enables secure access to stored credentials without hardcoding secrets in code or configuration files.

heading: account-manager Skill
content: Social account management — registration flows, profile optimization, credential storage, multi-platform account orchestration.. You are a social account management agent. You help set up, configure, and manage accounts across social media platforms — handling registration flows, profile optimization, credential storage, and multi-platform orchestration.

heading: agent-config Skill
content: Export and import agent configurations for sharing and backup. Export agent configurations as portable YAML/JSON files for sharing, backup, and migration. Import configurations to recreate agents.

heading: amazon-polly Skill
content: Neural text-to-speech via Amazon Polly — high-quality voices, MP3 output, voice listing, default Joanna (en-US Neural).. Use this skill when the agent is deployed on AWS infrastructure or when the user prefers Amazon Polly's Neural engine voices. Polly provides high-quality neural TTS with MP3 output and a wide range of voices across languages and regions.

heading: apple-notes Skill
content: Create, read, search, and manage notes in Apple Notes using AppleScript and macOS automation.. You can create, read, search, and manage notes in Apple Notes on macOS using AppleScript commands executed via `osascript`. This provides native access to the Notes app without requiring third-party tools or API keys.

heading: apple-reminders Skill
content: Create, manage, and query reminders and lists in Apple Reminders using AppleScript and macOS automation.. You can create, manage, complete, and query reminders in Apple Reminders on macOS using AppleScript commands executed via `osascript`. This provides native access to the Reminders app for task management without external services.

heading: audio-generation Skill
content: Music and sound effects generation — 8 providers with fallback chains, user-configurable preferences, local and cloud options.. Use this skill when the user wants to generate music compositions or sound effects from text descriptions. The system supports 8 provider backends with automatic fallback chains and user-configurable provider preferences.

heading: blog-publisher Skill
content: Blog publishing automation — long-form content creation, SEO optimization, cross-posting with canonical URLs, and multi-platform distribution.. You are an autonomous blog publishing agent. You create, optimize, and distribute long-form content across multiple blogging platforms — ensuring SEO best practices, consistent branding, and proper canonical URL management for cross-posted content.

heading: bluesky-bot Skill
content: Bluesky automation — decentralized social engagement, custom feed curation, authentic community building, and AT Protocol interaction.. You are an autonomous Bluesky engagement agent. You participate in the decentralized social web — posting authentic content, building community, curating custom feeds, and engaging with conversations without gaming any algorithms.

heading: channel-management Skill
content: Unified guide to all 37 messaging channel adapters — OAuth setup, channel selection by audience, and multi-channel posting strategy.. You are a channel routing specialist. You understand all 37 messaging channel adapters in the AgentOS ecosystem and know when to use each, how to configure OAuth, and how to orchestrate multi-channel operations.

heading: cli-tools Skill
content: Shell execution patterns, CLI discovery, filesystem operations, and subprocess management for autonomous agent workflows.. You have access to shell execution and filesystem tools. Use them effectively and safely.

heading: cloud-deployment Skill
content: Deploy to 9 cloud providers — AWS, Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Fly.io, Heroku, DigitalOcean, Linode, Cloudflare. Provider selection, deployment patterns, cost comparison.. You are a cloud deployment specialist. You know all 9 cloud provider extensions and can recommend the right one based on project type, scale, budget, and team requirements.

heading: cloud-ops Skill
content: Manage cloud infrastructure — monitor deployments, scale resources, manage databases, and handle domain operations across all supported providers.. Manage and monitor cloud infrastructure across all supported providers. Handle scaling, monitoring, database operations, DNS management, and troubleshooting.

heading: code-safety Skill
content: Scan LLM-generated code for security vulnerabilities using language-aware pattern rules. A guardrail automatically scans code in your responses for security vulnerabilities. You also have a tool for on-demand code scanning.

heading: coding-agent Skill
content: Write, review, debug, refactor, and explain code across multiple programming languages and frameworks.. You are a skilled software developer capable of writing, reviewing, debugging, refactoring, and explaining code across a wide range of programming languages and frameworks. Approach every coding task with attention to correctness, readability, maintainability, and performance.

heading: company-research Skill
content: Research companies and contacts using Clearbit enrichment API.. You can research companies and people using the Clearbit enrichment API.

You research topics, write engaging content for multiple formats (blog posts, social captions, video descriptions, newsletters), and optimize everything for SEO and engagement.

heading: deep-research Skill
content: Multi-source investigation — academic papers, web research, social media, cross-referencing, and comprehensive report generation.. You are a thorough research and investigation agent. You conduct multi-source investigations, verify claims across independent sources, search academic literature, and produce comprehensive research reports.

heading: diarization Skill
content: Speaker diarization — identifies and tracks who is speaking at each moment in an audio stream, using provider-delegated labels or local offline clustering.. Use this skill when the agent needs to track who is speaking across a multi-speaker audio stream. Supports two modes: provider-delegated (extracts speaker labels from Deepgram word-level results) and local clustering (spectral-centroid agglomerative clustering, fully offline).

heading: discord-helper Skill
content: Manage Discord servers, channels, roles, and messages through the Discord API.. You can interact with Discord servers (guilds) to send messages, manage channels, assign roles, moderate content, and handle events. Use the Discord API with the configured bot token to perform server management tasks.

heading: document-export Skill
content: Export research, reports, and analysis to PDF, DOCX, PPTX, CSV, and XLSX with professional formatting, charts, and theming.. You can export your responses, research, and analysis to professional documents in five formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, CSV, and XLSX.

heading: emergent-tools Skill

You have access to the EmergentCapabilityEngine — a system that lets you create brand-new tools at runtime when no existing tool satisfies the user's request, and a suite of self-improvement tools that let you adapt your personality, manage your skills, compose workflows, and evaluate your own performance. These are powerful capabilities; use them wisely.

heading: endpoint-semantic Skill
content: Semantic endpoint detection — uses an LLM to classify whether the user's utterance is a complete thought, reducing false turn boundaries on mid-sentence pauses.. This extension adds an LLM classifier step that distinguishes a genuine turn boundary from a thinking pause.

content: Facebook automation — community engagement, page management, visual content publishing, and audience growth.. You are an autonomous Facebook community engagement agent. heading: grounding-guard Skill
content: Verify response faithfulness against RAG source documents using NLI entailment and LLM-as-judge. A guardrail automatically verifies that your responses are faithful to the source documents retrieved via RAG. Claims in your output are checked against the retrieved sources using NLI entailment detection.

heading: healthcheck Skill
content: Monitor health and availability of systems, services, APIs, and infrastructure endpoints.. You can check the health and availability of systems, services, APIs, and infrastructure endpoints. heading: image-editing Skill
content: Edit, transform, and enhance images using AI models. Edit images with img2img transformation, inpainting (fill masked regions), outpainting (extend beyond borders), upscaling (2x/4x super resolution), and creating variations.

heading: image-gen Skill
content: Generate, edit, upscale, and variate images using the AgentOS multi-provider image pipeline with automatic fallback.. Use this skill when the user wants to create, edit, upscale, or create variations of images. AgentOS provides four high-level APIs that route to any configured provider with automatic fallback when multiple providers have credentials set.

content: Instagram growth automation — post scheduling, story creation, hashtag strategy, Reel uploads, and engagement analytics.. You are an autonomous Instagram content and engagement agent. content: Generate interactive HTML/CSS/JS widgets — data visualizations, 3D scenes, dashboards, maps, and more.. You can create interactive HTML/CSS/JS widgets that run in the browser. content: Cognitive memory management — encode, recall, forget, set reminders, and maintain long-term knowledge using personality-modulated memory.. heading: ml-content-classifier Skill

content: Research movies and TV shows using OMDB (IMDB/RT/Metacritic scores) and Letterboxd (community ratings and reviews).. You can research movies and TV shows by combining data from OMDB and Letterboxd.

heading: notion Skill

heading: openwakeword Skill

Use this skill to enable hands-free wake-word activation using open-source ONNX models. Unlike Porcupine, OpenWakeWord requires no API key or cloud account — it runs fully offline using `onnxruntime-node` and any ONNX-compatible wake-word model.

heading: pii-redaction Skill

You have access to PII detection and redaction capabilities. A guardrail automatically redacts PII from your inputs and outputs, but you can also proactively scan and redact text before storing it, sending it to external APIs, or sharing it across agents.

heading: pinterest-bot Skill

heading: piper Skill

Use this skill when the agent must synthesize speech without internet connectivity, or when user privacy requirements prohibit sending text to external TTS APIs. Piper runs as a local binary using an ONNX voice model, producing WAV audio with zero network dependencies.

heading: porcupine Skill

Use this skill to enable hands-free wake-word activation for a voice agent. The agent listens passively for a configured keyword (e.g. "hey porcupine", "bumblebee") and only becomes active when it hears the word, rather than continuously processing all audio.

heading: streaming-stt-deepgram Skill

Use this skill when the user needs real-time speech-to-text transcription with the lowest possible latency. Deepgram's WebSocket API provides sub-300 ms interim transcripts using the Nova-2 model.

heading: streaming-stt-whisper Skill
content: Chunked sliding-window streaming speech-to-text via OpenAI Whisper HTTP API — compatible with local Faster-Whisper, Groq, and OpenRouter endpoints.. Use this skill when OpenAI Whisper is the preferred STT provider, especially when a single API key should cover both LLM and speech. This provider uses a sliding-window ring buffer to simulate streaming over the file-based Whisper HTTP endpoint.

heading: streaming-tts-elevenlabs Skill
content: Real-time streaming text-to-speech via ElevenLabs WebSocket API — persistent connection, sentence-boundary flushing, turbo and multilingual models.. Use this skill when voice quality or voice cloning is the highest priority. ElevenLabs uses a persistent WebSocket connection per session and streams MP3 audio chunks as soon as each sentence boundary is reached, enabling near-instant audio playback with premium voice quality.

heading: streaming-tts-openai Skill
content: Low-latency streaming text-to-speech via OpenAI TTS API — adaptive sentence chunking, concurrent fetch pipelining, six voices.. Use this skill when the agent needs to synthesize speech from LLM output with low latency using OpenAI's TTS API. The provider buffers incoming tokens into sentence chunks before making API requests, enabling audio to begin playing within the first sentence rather than waiting for the full response.

heading: structured-output Skill
content: Extract structured JSON data from text using Zod schemas. Extract structured data from unstructured text using Zod schema validation. Supports generateObject (blocking) and streamObject (streaming with partial objects).

heading: summarize Skill
content: Summarize text content, web pages, documents, and long-form articles into concise, structured summaries.. You can summarize text content, web pages, articles, documents, and other long-form material into concise, structured summaries. Adapt the summary format and depth to the user's needs and the source material type.

heading: topicality Skill
content: Enforce allowed and forbidden conversation topics using semantic embedding similarity with session-aware drift detection. A guardrail automatically enforces conversation topic boundaries. Messages matching forbidden topics are blocked. Messages outside allowed topics are flagged. Gradual off-topic drift across multiple turns is detected.

content: Manage Trello boards, lists, cards, checklists, and team workflows via the Trello API.. You can manage Trello boards, lists, and cards to organize projects and track workflows. Use the Trello REST API with API key and token authentication to perform board operations programmatically.

You can post tweets, reply to conversations, create threads, engage with trending topics, and track your performance analytics.

content: Video generation, analysis, and scene detection — text-to-video, image-to-video, structured scene descriptions with RAG indexing, and general-purpose visual change detection.. Use this skill when the user wants to create AI-generated videos, analyse existing video content for structured scene descriptions, or detect visual changes in live/recorded frame streams.

heading: vision-ocr Skill
content: Extract text from images using OCR and vision AI with the performOCR() high-level API or the full VisionPipeline.. Extract text from images, documents, and handwritten notes using a progressive 3-tier pipeline: local OCR (PaddleOCR / Tesseract) -> local vision models (TrOCR, Florence-2) -> cloud vision LLM (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini).

Use this skill when the user wants an agent to listen, transcribe, respond with speech, or switch between speech providers without changing the rest of the workflow.

heading: voice-telephony Skill
content: Voice call routing with Twilio, Telnyx, and Plivo plus STT/TTS streaming providers — IVR setup, provider selection, and voice pipeline configuration.. You are a voice pipeline specialist. You configure telephony providers for call routing, set up IVR flows, and wire STT/TTS streaming providers for real-time voice conversations.

heading: vosk Skill
content: Fully offline speech-to-text via the Vosk library — streaming recognition, 16 kHz PCM, no network required after model download.. Use this skill when the agent must operate without internet connectivity, or when user privacy requirements prohibit sending audio to external APIs. Vosk provides fully offline speech recognition after the model is downloaded once.

heading: weather Skill
content: Look up current weather conditions, forecasts, and severe weather alerts for any location worldwide.. You can retrieve current weather conditions, multi-day forecasts, and severe weather alerts for any location the user specifies. Use the web-search tool to query weather data from reputable sources such as weather.gov, OpenWeatherMap, or AccuWeather.

heading: web-scraper Skill
content: Autonomous web scraping — navigate sites, extract structured data, handle pagination, anti-detection, and proxy rotation.. You are an autonomous web scraping agent. You navigate websites, extract structured data, handle pagination, manage sessions, and deal with anti-bot measures — all using the browser automation tools.

heading: web-search Skill
content: Search the web for up-to-date information, news, documentation, and answers to questions.. You have access to a web search tool that lets you find current information from across the internet. Use it proactively whenever the user asks about recent events, statistics, documentation, or anything that may have changed since your training cutoff.

heading: whisper-transcribe Skill
content: Transcribe audio and video files to text using OpenAI Whisper or compatible speech-to-text APIs.. You can transcribe audio and video files into text using OpenAI's Whisper API or compatible speech-to-text services. Support a wide range of audio formats (mp3, mp4, wav, m4a, webm, flac, ogg) with automatic language detection and optional translation to English.

heading: youtube-bot Skill
content: YouTube automation — video publishing, Shorts creation, community engagement, playlist management, and channel growth analytics.. You are an autonomous YouTube content and engagement agent. You manage a professional YouTube presence — uploading videos and Shorts, engaging with comments, curating playlists, and growing your channel through SEO-optimized content and community interaction.

heading: How do I add voice to my agent?
content: Use the voice pipeline: agent({ model: 'openai:gpt-4o', voice: { stt: 'openai', tts: 'elevenlabs' } }). Requires OPENAI_API_KEY and ELEVENLABS_API_KEY. See the voice-conversation skill for full setup.

heading: What LLM models are supported?
content: AgentOS supports 21 LLM providers: OpenAI (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini), Anthropic (claude-sonnet, claude-haiku, claude-opus), Google (gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash), Ollama (any local model), OpenRouter (800+ models), plus Groq, Together, Fireworks, Mistral, Cohere, Perplexity, DeepSeek, xAI, and more.

heading: How do I add tools to my agent?
content: Pass tools array to agent(): agent({ model: 'openai:gpt-4o', tools: [myTool] }). Tools implement { name, description, inputSchema, execute }. Use createCuratedManifest() from @framers/agentos-extensions-registry to load pre-built extension packs.

heading: How do I deploy an AgentOS agent?
content: AgentOS agents are standard Node.js processes. Deploy via Docker, systemd, or any container orchestrator. For serverless, wrap the agent in an HTTP handler (Express, Fastify, or Next.js API route). Use the cloud-deployment skill for guided setup on AWS, GCP, or Azure.

heading: How does agent memory work?
content: AgentOS provides working memory (in-context), episodic memory (conversation history), semantic memory (vector-indexed knowledge), and procedural memory (skills). Use the MemoryManager API or the memory-manager skill. Memory is backed by sql-storage-adapter for persistence.

heading: How do I add guardrails to my agent?
content: Import from '@framers/agentos/safety/guardrails'. Use ContentGuardrail for input/output filtering, PII redaction via openredaction, and SecurityTiers (dangerous/permissive/balanced/strict/paranoid) for tool access control. Guardrails can be chained in a pipeline.

heading: What messaging channels are supported?
content: AgentOS supports 37 channel platforms: Discord, Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, Twitter/X, Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky, Mastodon, Reddit, YouTube, TikTok, Pinterest, Farcaster, Lemmy, Google Business, email, SMS, and more. Each channel has a dedicated adapter implementing IChannelAdapter.

heading: What are skills and how do I use them?
content: Skills are markdown instruction files (SKILL.md) that teach an agent domain-specific behavior. Load skills via agent.config.json or programmatically with SkillLoader. There are 69+ curated skills in @framers/agentos-skills covering web search, coding, social media, voice, and more.

heading: Can I create multi-agent systems?
content: Yes. Use the orchestration module for multi-agent workflows: import { AgentGraph, MissionAPI } from '@framers/agentos/orchestration'. AgentGraph defines agent-to-agent communication topology. MissionAPI manages goal decomposition and task delegation across agents.

heading: What are emergent tools?
content: Emergent tools are dynamically generated tool implementations that the agent creates at runtime to solve novel problems. The emergent-tools skill enables this behavior. The agent writes, validates, and executes tool code in a sandboxed environment.

heading: Can AgentOS generate or analyze video?
content: Yes. Use generateVideo() for AI video generation and analyzeVideo() for frame-by-frame analysis. Import from '@framers/agentos'. Video generation supports multiple providers. Scene detection and temporal analysis are available via the vision module.

heading: Can AgentOS generate audio and music?
content: Yes. Use generateMusic() for music and generateSFX() for sound effects. Import from '@framers/agentos'. Both support multiple providers and return audio buffers or URLs. The audio-generation skill provides guided workflows.

heading: How do I generate or edit images?
content: Use generateImage() for creation and editImage() for modification. Import from '@framers/agentos'. Supports DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, and other providers. The image-gen and image-editing skills provide guided workflows.

heading: How do I extract text from images or PDFs?
content: Use performOCR() from '@framers/agentos'. Supports Tesseract.js (local) and PaddleOCR (via ppu-paddle-ocr). For PDFs, the unpdf dependency handles text extraction. The vision-ocr skill combines OCR with scene understanding.

heading: How do I integrate with GitHub?
content: Use the GitHub extension pack from @framers/agentos-ext-github. Provides tools for repository management, issue tracking, PR review, code search, and ecosystem indexing. Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var. The QueryRouter can auto-index GitHub repos for knowledge retrieval.

heading: What are security tiers?
content: AgentOS has 5 security tiers: dangerous (no restrictions), permissive (basic checks), balanced (default — blocks known-dangerous tools), strict (allowlist only), paranoid (human-in-the-loop for everything). Set via SecurityTiers in '@framers/agentos/safety/runtime'.

heading: How do I set provider preferences?
content: Use LLMProviderConfig: { apiKey, model, baseUrl?, extraHeaders? }. Set per-call via generateText({ apiKey, model }) or globally via env vars (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.). OpenRouter fallback is automatic when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is set.

heading: Can agents improve themselves over time?
content: Yes. AgentOS supports style adaptation (learns user communication preferences), cognitive memory mechanisms (reconsolidation, gist extraction, schema encoding), and skill acquisition. The StyleAdaptation module tracks formality, verbosity, and technicality preferences.

heading: What is the QueryRouter?
content: The QueryRouter is an intelligent RAG pipeline that classifies queries by complexity (T0-T3), retrieves relevant context via vector search or keyword fallback, and generates grounded answers with citations. Import from '@framers/agentos/query-router'.

heading: Does AgentOS support streaming?
content: Yes. Use streamText() for token-by-token streaming: import { streamText } from '@framers/agentos'. Returns an async iterable of text chunks. Works with all LLM providers that support streaming (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.).

heading: How do I get structured/JSON output from the LLM?
content: Use the structured-output skill or pass a Zod schema to generateText(): generateText({ model: 'openai:gpt-4o', prompt: '...', schema: z.object({ name: z.string() }) }). The LLM output is validated and parsed automatically.

heading: How do I evaluate agent performance?
content: Use the fullstack-evals-harness-example package for systematic evaluation. Define datasets, graders, and candidates. Run experiments that measure quality across dataset x candidate x grader combinations. Compare baseline vs challenger with the comparison endpoint.

heading: How does source citation work?
content: The QueryRouter generates answers with inline source citations. Each citation references a CorpusChunk with its sourcePath. Citations include confidence scores. The generator prompt instructs the LLM to ground every claim in retrieved context.

heading: What are extensions and how do they differ from tools?
content: Extensions are packaged bundles of tools, skills, and channel adapters. Each extension implements ExtensionPack with a createExtensionPack() factory. Extensions are registered in @framers/agentos-extensions-registry. Tools are individual callable functions; extensions group related tools together.

heading: How do I use Ollama for local LLM inference?
content: Set model to 'ollama:<model-name>' (e.g., 'ollama:llama3'). Ollama must be running locally on port 11434. No API key required. Supports all generateText/streamText/agent APIs. Use SmallModelResolver for automatic model selection across providers.

heading: How do I build a RAG pipeline?
content: Use the RAG module: import from '@framers/agentos/rag'. Components include EmbeddingManager, VectorStoreManager, HybridSearcher (vector + keyword), RaptorTree (hierarchical summarization), and GraphRAG. The QueryRouter wraps all of these into a batteries-included pipeline.

heading: Can AgentOS handle phone calls?
content: Yes. Use the telephony module: import from '@framers/agentos/telephony'. Supports Twilio and VAPI for inbound/outbound voice calls with real-time STT/TTS. The voice-telephony skill provides a guided setup workflow.

heading: How do I redact PII from agent conversations?
content: Use the openredaction integration in the safety module, or the pii-redaction skill. Automatically detects and redacts names, emails, phone numbers, SSNs, credit cards, and addresses. Can be applied as a guardrail pre-filter or post-filter.

heading: How do I create a custom tool?
content: Implement the ITool interface: { name: string, description: string, inputSchema: JSONSchema, execute: (input) => Promise<result> }. Register with agent({ tools: [myTool] }) or via an ExtensionPack. Input is validated against inputSchema automatically.

heading: What are cognitive memory mechanisms?
content: AgentOS implements 8 research-backed memory mechanisms: reconsolidation, retrieval-induced forgetting (RIF), involuntary recall, feeling-of-knowing (FOK), temporal gist extraction, schema encoding, source confidence decay, and emotion regulation. All are HEXACO personality-modulated.

heading: generateText() API
content: import { generateText } from '@framers/agentos'. generateText({ model: 'openai:gpt-4o', prompt: 'Hello', system?: '...', tools?: [...], maxSteps?: 5, temperature?: 0.7 }) -> Promise<{ text, provider, model, usage, toolCalls }>. The core text generation function supporting all 21 LLM providers.

heading: streamText() API
content: import { streamText } from '@framers/agentos'. streamText({ model: 'openai:gpt-4o', prompt: 'Hello', system?: '...' }) -> AsyncIterable<string>. Token-by-token streaming for real-time output. Supports all providers with streaming capability.

heading: agent() API
content: import { agent } from '@framers/agentos'. agent({ model: 'openai:gpt-4o', instructions: '...', tools?: [...], maxSteps?: 5 }) -> { generate(prompt), stream(prompt), session(id?) }. Creates a reusable agent with built-in tool orchestration and session management.

heading: generateImage() API
content: import { generateImage } from '@framers/agentos'. generateImage({ model: 'openai:dall-e-3', prompt: 'A sunset over mountains', size?: '1024x1024', n?: 1 }) -> Promise<{ images: Array<{ url, base64? }> }>. AI image generation supporting DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, and more.

heading: editImage() API
content: import { editImage } from '@framers/agentos'. editImage({ model: 'openai:dall-e-2', image: Buffer, prompt: 'Add a rainbow', mask?: Buffer }) -> Promise<{ images: Array<{ url }> }>. Modify existing images using AI inpainting and editing.

heading: generateVideo() API
content: import { generateVideo } from '@framers/agentos'. generateVideo({ prompt: 'A cat walking', duration?: 5, fps?: 24 }) -> Promise<{ url, frames? }>. AI video generation from text prompts.

heading: analyzeVideo() API
content: import { analyzeVideo } from '@framers/agentos'. analyzeVideo({ url: '...', tasks: ['describe', 'detect-scenes'] }) -> Promise<{ description, scenes, frames }>. Multi-modal video analysis with scene detection.

heading: generateMusic() API
content: import { generateMusic } from '@framers/agentos'. generateMusic({ prompt: 'Upbeat jazz', duration?: 30 }) -> Promise<{ audio: Buffer, url? }>. AI music generation from text descriptions.

heading: generateSFX() API
content: import { generateSFX } from '@framers/agentos'. generateSFX({ prompt: 'Thunder crack', duration?: 5 }) -> Promise<{ audio: Buffer, url? }>. AI sound effect generation.

heading: performOCR() API
content: import { performOCR } from '@framers/agentos'. performOCR({ image: Buffer | string, engine?: 'tesseract' | 'paddle' }) -> Promise<{ text, blocks, confidence }>. Extract text from images using Tesseract.js or PaddleOCR.

heading: detectScenes() API
content: import { detectScenes } from '@framers/agentos'. detectScenes({ video: Buffer | string, threshold?: 0.3 }) -> Promise<{ scenes: Array<{ start, end, description }> }>. Temporal scene boundary detection in video content.

heading: QueryRouter class API
content: import { QueryRouter } from '@framers/agentos/query-router'. new QueryRouter({ knowledgeCorpus: ['./docs'], generationModel: 'gpt-4o-mini' }). Methods: init(), route(query, history?), close(), getCorpusStats(), getEmbeddingStatus(). Intelligent RAG pipeline with classification, retrieval, and grounded generation.

heading: MemoryManager class API
content: import { MemoryManager } from '@framers/agentos/memory'. Manages working memory, episodic memory, semantic memory, and procedural memory. Methods: encode(content), recall(query, topK?), forget(id), getWorkingMemory(), consolidate(). Backed by sql-storage-adapter.

heading: SkillLoader class API
content: import { SkillLoader } from '@framers/agentos/skills'. Loads SKILL.md files and injects their instructions into the agent system prompt. Methods: load(name), loadAll(names), resolve(path). Skills are sourced from @framers/agentos-skills curated registry.

heading: Error: No API key for openai
content: Set OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable or pass apiKey in options: generateText({ apiKey: 'sk-...', model: 'openai:gpt-4o', prompt: '...' }). For other providers, set the corresponding env var (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, etc.).

heading: Error: Top-level await not supported with cjs
content: AgentOS requires ESM. Add "type": "module" to your package.json, or use .mts file extension. If using TypeScript, set "module": "ESNext" or "NodeNext" in tsconfig.json.

heading: Error: Model not found or unsupported
content: Check the model string format: 'provider:model-name' (e.g., 'openai:gpt-4o', 'anthropic:claude-sonnet'). For Ollama, ensure the model is pulled locally: ollama pull <model-name>. For OpenRouter, prefix with 'openrouter:'.

heading: Error: QueryRouter init failed: no readable markdown corpus
content: The QueryRouter could not find any .md or .mdx files in the configured knowledgeCorpus directories. Verify the paths exist and contain markdown files with at least one heading and some content. Paths are resolved relative to process.cwd().

heading: Warning: Embedding initialization failed, falling back to keyword search
content: This is non-fatal. The QueryRouter uses keyword-based retrieval when vector embeddings are unavailable. To enable embeddings, set OPENAI_API_KEY and ensure the text-embedding-3-small model is accessible. Check network connectivity and API key permissions.

heading: Error: 429 Too Many Requests / Rate limit exceeded
content: You are hitting the LLM provider rate limit. Solutions: reduce maxSteps, add delays between calls, switch to a provider with higher limits, or use OpenRouter which distributes across multiple providers. For embeddings, batch your corpus loading.

heading: Error: Cannot find module hnswlib-node
content: hnswlib-node is an optional peer dependency for vector search. Install it: npm install hnswlib-node. On Apple Silicon, you may need: npm install hnswlib-node --build-from-source. The QueryRouter falls back to keyword search if it's not installed.

heading: Error: Cannot find module better-sqlite3
content: better-sqlite3 is an optional dependency for local storage. Install it: npm install better-sqlite3. On some platforms, you may need build tools (python3, make, g++). Alternatively, use @framers/sql-storage-adapter with a Postgres backend.

heading: NestJS app hangs on startup with Discord bot enabled
content: Socket.IO @WebSocketGateway can cause NestFactory.create() to hang. Ensure WunderlandGateway is disabled (empty providers array). Also avoid explicitly importing WunderlandSolModule — it is loaded transitively and duplicate imports cause hangs.

heading: How to configure OpenRouter as a fallback
content: Set OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable. AgentOS automatically detects it and uses OpenRouter as a fallback when the primary provider key is missing. OpenRouter supports 800+ models from multiple providers through a single API key.

heading: Error: Tool input validation failed
content: Tool inputs are validated against the inputSchema (JSON Schema). Check that your tool call arguments match the schema types and required fields. Use ajv-formats for string format validation (email, uri, date-time, etc.).

heading: Error: Tool blocked by security tier
content: The current security tier does not allow this tool. Check the agent's security tier setting in SecurityTiers. Use 'permissive' for development, 'balanced' for production, 'strict' for sensitive environments. Dangerous tools are blocked at 'balanced' and above.

heading: Out of memory when loading large corpus
content: Large markdown corpora can exceed Node.js heap limits. Solutions: increase heap with --max-old-space-size=4096, reduce corpus size, use the maxContextTokens config to limit retrieval context, or enable streaming retrieval with the UnifiedRetriever.

heading: NestJS decorator metadata not emitted with tsx/esbuild
content: @Injectable() parameter decorator metadata is not emitted by esbuild, causing constructor DI params to be undefined. Use process.env directly or @Inject() explicitly. For the backend, run with: npx tsx --tsconfig tsconfig.json src/main.ts.

heading: Git submodule changes not visible after commit
content: Always push submodule commits immediately after making them. Run git push in the submodule directory before committing the parent repo. The parent only tracks the submodule commit hash, not the content.
